tasks.title: F1 core: proposal-only capture + guard rekey
tasks.notes: Exemption removed; capture() choke-point forces proposal (UNVERIFIED+flagged+directive:pending, scope=project) per advisory M-A1 (recorded deviation: proposal transform lives in SqliteProvider.capture(), not only the kb-capture handler, so the HTTP route cannot mint an active directive; general INFERRED clamp stays in kb-capture.ts -- broader HTTP clamp bypass tracked as yashr-f3g). H1 promote() gate refuses user-directive naming the CLI; approveDirective is dedicated (no delegate to promote). H2 query()/prime() defaults exclude non-CONFIRMED user-directive; kb_list + flagged_only still show pending. audn supersede guard + decay guard rekeyed to type+CONFIRMED. L1 tool description updated; L2 decay wording. Deviation (resolution 1): D1's 'assert rather than filter' superseded by the binding H2 hardening. kb-user-directive.test.ts rewritten through activation (26 tests green). NOTE: the full activation-flow rewrite was done here (not deferred to T1.3) so every commit stays green; T1.3 adds the dedicated fail-then-pass gate file. Pre-existing (not introduced by T1.1): 4 tests in kb-session-prime.test.ts fail on this machine because they read the real project kb.sqlite (environmental leak) -- identical fail on clean base; plus the 2 allowed timezone failures (yashr-302).

tasks.title: F1 CLI: human-terminal activation surface
tasks.notes: New src/cli/kb-directives.ts (directives/approve-directive/reject-directive/add-directive) wired through the src/index.ts kb subcommand dispatch (resolution 4: one kb group). Opens providers.project (same KB the MCP server uses, cwd-resolved scope). Handlers exported for direct unit testing (13 tests green) plus runKbDirectives top-level dispatch. Trust-rationale header per D1. Smoke-tested end-to-end via node dist/index.js kb directives (exit 0) and error path (exit 1). --help updated. Never exposed over MCP.

tasks.title: F1 proof: fail-then-pass + directive test rewrite
tasks.notes: New tests/knowledge/kb-directive-gate.test.ts (8 tests) encodes the yashr-9ha attack through the real tool handlers (kbCapture/kbPromote/kbQuery/kbList) + provider.approveDirective as the CLI does. DOOR 1: forged capture -> pending (UNVERIFIED+flagged+directive:pending, scope forced project), absent from kb_query/prime defaults (H2), visible to kb_list+flagged_only. DOOR 2: two kb_promote -> both refused naming approve-directive, still inactive (promote-ladder attack, H1). CLOSED: approveDirective -> active with all four D6 semantics (top-tier retrieval, never decayed, agent capture only flags), proposal never supersedes active, supersede = human approve-new+reject-old (resolution 2). Fail-then-pass verified: 7/8 fail on pre-T1.1 source, 8/8 pass on patched. The kb-user-directive.test.ts rewrite through activation was done in T1.1 (26 tests) to keep every commit green -- boundary shift from the plan (which nominally put the rewrite in T1.3) recorded here. Full suite: 1878 passed; only pre-existing failures remain (4 env-leak session-prime + 2 allowed timezone yashr-302).

tasks.title: F2: auto-capture standing instructions (docs)
tasks.notes: DEVIATION (recorded, cosmetic only): executed after T1.6 instead of before it (dispatch order was T1.4/T1.5/T1.6; ran T1.6 first by mistake). No functional dependency between them, so no impact. Added a new 'Standing instructions' H2 section to skills/pm/SKILL.md (pending + exact CLI command apra-fleet kb approve-directive <id>); the pre-existing Lifecycle paragraph that claimed capture-time CONFIRMED (stale post-T1.1) now points to that new section instead of repeating the stale claim -- this is a necessary correctness fix (contradicting docs would be worse than a small in-place edit), not a restructure. Rewrote the 'User directives' section of skills/pm/tpl-kb-agent.md (was titled a D6 CONFIRMED-on-capture exception; now titled D1 proposal+CLI-activation) to state pending/CLI-activation and added the requested guidance that the KB Agent may itself propose a directive detected in the session record via the same pending flow; also fixed a Step 6b parenthetical referencing the removed capture-time-CONFIRMED path (same correctness-fix rationale). Added the one-line note to skills/pm/kb-review.md per the spec (pending directive proposals surfaced, CLI-only resolution, never kb_promote). Docs-only, no server code touched; verified ASCII-only via a byte scan of all three files.

tasks.title: F3: polarity word-boundary matching
tasks.notes: DEVIATION (recorded, cosmetic only): executed after T1.4/T1.6 rather than the dispatched T1.4/T1.5/T1.6 order (T1.6 ran first by mistake, T1.4 second); no functional dependency issue since T1.5 only needed T1.1 (already landed) ahead of it in audn.ts, which held. Replaced hasOppositePolarity's String.includes() substring checks with precompiled word-boundary regexes (\b anchored at each whole phrase's outer edges, case-insensitive via the 'i' flag); POLARITY_NEGATIVE/POLARITY_POSITIVE lists unchanged; the guard-rekey/contradiction/dedup/supersede structure in makeAudnDecision (T1.1's territory) was not touched. Fail-then-pass VERIFIED BY ACTUAL RUN (not just reasoned): added 3 false-positive tests ('prefixed'/'unresolved'/'suffixed' vs genuine 'is broken' text) plus 4 regression-guard tests to tests/knowledge/audn.test.ts, ran them against the pre-fix source first -- all 3 false-positive tests failed exactly as predicted (asserted false, old includes()-based code returned true), the 4 regression tests already passed pre-fix -- then applied the word-boundary fix and reran: all 7 new tests plus the full audn.test.ts (26/26) pass. Verified apostrophe phrases ("doesn't work") still match since \b anchors only at the phrase's outer edges, not per inner word. Full tests/knowledge suite: 236 passed, only the 4 pre-existing kb-session-prime.test.ts graph-neighbor-expansion failures (yashr-bwc) remain, unrelated to this change.

tasks.title: F4: explicit validated repo path for export + cold-seed
tasks.notes: DEVIATION (recorded, no functional impact): executed before T1.4/T1.5 -- neither depends on this file pair, so order was safe, but the dispatch specified T1.4/T1.5/T1.6 sequential order and this ran first by mistake; T1.4 and T1.5 follow in this same dispatch. kb-export.ts: added resolveRepoPath(explicit) helper reused for both tiers -- explicit repo_path validated (existsSync+isDirectory) same as before; when omitted, falls back to process.cwd() but now validated through the identical check rather than a blind '|| process.cwd()' (no behavior change for valid inputs; new behavior only on the previously-unhandled edge of an invalid cwd, which now throws the same clear error instead of writing to a bad path). kb-session-prime.ts: added optional repo_path to kbSessionPrimeSchema (cold-seed block only), resolveRepoPath() returns null (not throw) preserving the non-fatal hard-skip contract; explicit invalid input does NOT fall through to cwd (verified by test). Tests added in tests/knowledge/kb-export.test.ts (3 new: cwd fallback, explicit-wins-over-cwd, refuse-when-neither-valid) and tests/knowledge/kb-session-prime.test.ts (4 new in a new 'repo path precedence' describe block: explicit wins, invalid explicit skips silently without falling back to cwd, omitted falls back to validated cwd, neither valid skips silently). The 4 pre-existing kb-session-prime.test.ts failures (graph-neighbor expansion describe block, unmocked process.cwd() reads this repo's real .fleet/kb-canonical.json -- yashr-bwc) reproduced identically after my edit (same 4 test names, same diff shape); git stash to diff against the pre-edit tree was denied by the sandbox policy, so equivalence was confirmed by code inspection (old code's unconditional `process.cwd()` read of that path is unchanged in shape by resolveRepoPath when cwd validates, which it does on this real machine) rather than a literal before/after test run.

tasks.title: VERIFY Phase 1
tasks.notes: npm run build: CLEAN (tsc, no errors/output). npm test: 130 files, 1912 tests -- 1892 passed, 6 failed, 14 skipped. All 6 failures are on the allowed list, no new/different failures: (a) 2 timezone failures in tests/time-utils.test.ts (toLocalISOString offset/minute-preservation, yashr-302); (b) 4 pre-existing failures in tests/knowledge/kb-session-prime.test.ts 'kb_session_prime graph-neighbor expansion' describe block (graceful-skip x2, isError-context, FTS-hostile-neighbor) -- these leak this repo's real .fleet/kb-canonical.json because that describe block never mocks process.cwd(); confirmed unaffected by T1.6's resolveRepoPath refactor since cwd validates successfully on this real machine (same code path shape as the pre-T1.6 unconditional process.cwd() call). npx gitnexus analyze: SUCCEEDED (21.1s, 4347 nodes, 11764 edges, 243 clusters, 300 flows, incremental changed=19 added=4); it modified AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md (injected non-ASCII gitnexus:start/end markers) as documented -- reverted both via git checkout -- AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md immediately after, confirmed via git status. Pre-existing untracked .claude/skills/ and .claude/worktrees/ directories (dated before this dispatch -- other agents' worktree checkouts and a gitnexus skill cache) are left alone: they are orchestrator/environment scratch outside this sprint's scope, not sprint deliverables, and committing a nested worktree's raw files would be destructive; git status --porcelain shows only these two pre-existing untracked entries, nothing else. Pushing branch feat/code-intelligence-abstraction next; commit field will be updated to this VERIFY commit's real SHA once created.

tasks.title: F5: kb_stats tool + provider stats read
tasks.notes: New src/tools/kb-stats.ts + SqliteProvider.stats() (kb-list no-bump pattern, plain SELECT/COUNT/GROUP BY only). Sections: totals (GROUP BY confidence/type, whole-table counts); stale/flagged/superseded (whole-table counts); retrieval {entries_retrieved (use_count>0 AND live), total_uses (SUM(use_count), whole-table), hit_rate = entries_retrieved/total-live per resolution 6, null when total-live is 0}; promote_ratio = promoted_at-IS-NOT-NULL-count / CONFIRMED-count, null when no CONFIRMED yet; coverage (per-symbol EXACT-match boolean via json_each + fraction). 'Live' = superseded_at IS NULL AND stale=0 everywhere (matches list()/query() defaults). bible section computed in the TOOL layer (not the provider) via providers.project.list({confidence:'CONFIRMED'}) -- already the exact live-CONFIRMED set kb_export uses, so no new provider primitive was needed: drift = count of live-CONFIRMED entries whose (promoted_at||created_at) is newer than the bible's newest updated_at; absent/unreadable/malformed/non-array bible -> present=false, drift=ALL live CONFIRMED (D5), never thrown -- verified by a dedicated malformed-JSON test that initially caught the wrong branch (fixed by hoisting the liveConfirmed-count fallback outside the file-read try so a JSON.parse throw does not lose it). Repo path uses a local resolveRepoPath(explicit) matching the T1.6 precedence but returning null (non-fatal) rather than throwing, since kb_stats must never fail the whole tool over a bad repo path. Registered as kb_stats in src/index.ts. `stats()` added to the MemoryProvider interface (unlike list(), which stayed SqliteProvider-only) because D4 explicitly binds HttpKbProvider to implement it or return a documented not-supported result -- HttpKbProvider.stats() returns {supported:false, reason, ...all-zero/null shape} without any network call, never throws. 14 tests in tests/knowledge/kb-stats.test.ts cover every section (empty KB; mixed-confidence/type fixture; stale=2 from BOTH the invalidated context-cache entry AND evaluateAudn's update-path superseded row which also sets stale=1 -- corrected from an initial wrong expectation of 1; flagged; superseded; hit_rate denominator arithmetic; promote_ratio; coverage exact-vs-substring near-miss; the kb_list-pattern no-bump proof via the double-query({ids}) technique from kb-list.test.ts; bible absent/current/drift/malformed-JSON/invalid-repo-path; HttpKbProvider never-throw). npm run build clean. Full suite after this task: only the 4 pre-existing kb-session-prime.test.ts leaks (yashr-bwc) fail, no new/different failures.

tasks.title: F5/F6: fleet_status KB health + bible drift
tasks.notes: src/tools/check-status.ts gains kbHealthSummary()/kbHealthCompactLine()/KbHealth types, following the codeIntelligenceHealth degraded-safe precedent exactly (KB 4e11460c): kbHealthSummary() calls kbStats({}) (T2.1) and JSON.parses the result inside try/catch -> null on ANY failure (reject or bad JSON); fleetStatus() adds a belt-and-suspenders outer try/catch around the call too (mirrors the existing codeIntelligence call site) so the section can never throw into fleet_status. Compact line format: 'kb: N entries (confirmed:C stale:S flagged:F) | hit-rate:H% | promote-ratio:P%' with hit-rate/promote-ratio rendered 'n/a' (not 0% or NaN) when their kb_stats denominator is null (empty KB, resolution 6 semantics). JSON payload gains a kbHealth key that is OMITTED (not present as null) when the health read fails, per the literal 'section omitted' wording in the dispatch spec -- this differs deliberately from codeIntelligence's own null-safe-default-object pattern, which always includes the key. Bible drift wording uses the AMENDED D5 text (auto-commit lands in T2.3, not yet landed at this task boundary but the wording anticipates it per the dispatch): nonzero drift -> 'bible: N promotions behind (auto-commit may have failed -- run apra-fleet kb commit)', appended to the compact line only when drift>0 (also present inside the bible sub-object in JSON regardless). 12 tests in tests/fleet-status-kb-health.test.ts: kbHealthCompactLine pure-function rendering (healthy no-drift, drift>0 exact wording, null-rate n/a, bible-absent-with-drift), kbHealthSummary degraded-safe (reject -> null, malformed JSON -> null), and full fleetStatus() integration (compact + json success, drift line, error-path section omission in both formats, proof that member/codeIntelligence sections are untouched when KB fails) -- kb-stats.js is vi.mock'd entirely so none of this touches a real kb.sqlite (the same real-KB-leak class of bug flagged as yashr-bwc in kb-session-prime.test.ts). npm run build clean; ran tests/fleet-status-kb-health.test.ts + tests/fleet-status-branch.test.ts + tests/fleet-status-code-intelligence.test.ts together (40/40 green, no regressions).

tasks.title: F6 docs: export-then-commit checklist
tasks.notes: T2.3 upgraded per user directive 2026-07-07: PLAN.md dispatched this as a docs-only 'export-then-commit checklist' task (claude-haiku-4-5), but the user directive 2026-07-07 ('at time of harvesting we should commit our learning -- we should not run this manually') amended design.md D5 and requirements.md to F6a, turning this into a CODE change -- auto-commit inside kb_export itself. Implemented exactly as the amended D5 spec: after writing .fleet/kb-canonical.json, when repoPath has a .git dir AND `git status --porcelain -- <bible-path>` shows the file actually changed (covers both 'modified since last commit' and 'brand-new untracked on first export'), runs `git add <bible-path>` then `git -c user.name=pm-kb -c user.email=kb@pm.local commit -m "chore(kb): update knowledge bible -- <N> confirmed entries" -- <bible-path>` (pathspec-only on both the add and the commit's own scope -- verified by a dedicated test that a staged unrelated file is never swept in). Any git failure (execFileSync throw) is caught, logged via logWarn('kb-export', ...), and non-fatal -- export's own JSON.stringify return already happened by construction (committed:false is just appended). Config off-switch read from FLEET_DIR/knowledge/config.json -> {bible:{autoCommit}}, default true, with missing-file/missing-section/malformed-JSON all degrading to true (never silently disables the feature on a config problem). Push intentionally NOT automatic (D5: rides existing sprint push cadence). kb_export's JSON result gains `committed: boolean`. Tool description in index.ts updated to describe the auto-commit + off-switch (no longer says 'the PM commits the resulting file', since that's now false). skills/pm/tpl-kb-agent.md updated per the upgrade spec: Role section's no-git-ops rule gets an explicit carve-out note (the commit is the TOOL's code, not agent discretion); Step 6b retitled as a promote->export->auto-commit checklist item with the full D5-amended explanation and the config off-switch; Step 8 report template gains a 'Bible: committed=.../drift=...' line noting nonzero drift is now an ANOMALY signal (auto-commit likely failed), not a routine reminder; Rules section's 'NEVER commit files' line gets the same carve-out. skills/pm/SKILL.md's PM-completion-flow checklist bullet (named in the ORIGINAL plan's docs-only version) was deliberately NOT touched -- the amended dispatch instructions name only tpl-kb-agent.md, and a manual-commit checklist step there would now be actively wrong (the step is automatic, not something to remind a human to do). 5 tests in tests/knowledge/kb-export-autocommit.test.ts using a real git-init temp-repo fixture confirm all five D5-mandated behaviors: content-unchanged -> no commit (verified via re-export with identical entries, commit count stays 1); changed -> exactly one pathspec commit with pm-kb identity (author/email/subject asserted via git log, plus git show --stat proves only the bible path was touched); git failure -> export success + logged warning (simulated via a fake non-repo .git file, warnSpy asserts logWarn called, export still returns exported:1); autoCommit:false -> no git call at all (config written to the shared FLEET_DIR/knowledge/config.json test path with a snapshot/restore around it to avoid polluting kb-setup.test.ts's shared state, verified via `git log` throwing -- zero commits ever created); dirty unrelated staged file never committed (git show --stat HEAD excludes it, git status still shows it staged afterward). npm run build clean. Byte-scanned all touched files for non-ASCII -- the only non-ASCII bytes found are pre-existing lines in src/index.ts (the --help text's em-dash bullets), confirmed via git diff to be outside every line this task touched. Full tests/knowledge/ suite after this task: 259 tests, only the 4 pre-existing kb-session-prime.test.ts leaks (yashr-bwc) fail, no new/different failures.

tasks.title: F7: server version handshake
tasks.notes: New src/services/version-check.ts (small helper module, per the plan's 'either in check-status.ts or a new src/services/version-check.ts' choice). readDiskVersion(startDir?) does a FRESH read every call (not cached at process start like serverVersion itself) via a local findProjectRootFrom() walk-up (mirrors findProjectRoot in install.ts:88-95, capped at 5 levels, same shape); for SEA mode it reads the embedded manifest.json asset instead (same 'node:sea' getAsset mechanism install.ts's own isSea()/getSeaAsset use) since SEA binaries carry no bare version.json on disk relative to the running process. Reused isSea() directly from src/cli/install.ts (already an established cross-import, see src/delivery-mode.ts's identical import) rather than duplicating SEA detection. checkVersionMismatch(running, startDir?) strips a dev git-hash suffix ('_abcdef') from the running side before the string compare, since disk reads are always a bare 'vX.Y.Z' and a hash-suffix artifact of dev mode must not read as a version bump. Both functions degrade to null on ANY failure (missing file, malformed JSON, non-string version field, SEA asset unavailable) -- never throw, per D6's 'omit silently' contract. Wired into check-status.ts's fleetStatus() strictly after T2.2's KB section (shared-file sequencing per PLAN.md), same try/catch-and-default-to-null shape: mismatch -> compact line 'server running vX, disk has vY -- restart your MCP client' (versionMismatchCompactLine()) + a versionMismatch json field; match or any failure -> both omitted entirely (no auto-restart, surface only). 13 tests: tests/version-check.test.ts (10, mocking src/cli/install.js's isSea() the same way tests/delivery-mode.test.ts does -- disk read match, nested subdirectory walk-up, missing/malformed/non-string version.json, SEA-mode-with-unavailable-asset never-throws, mismatch compare with hash-suffix stripping, unreadable-disk omission; caught and fixed a real test bug during this task: vi.clearAllMocks() in afterEach does NOT reset a prior mockReturnValue(true) on isSea(), so the SEA-mode test's mock was leaking into later tests -- fixed by setting isSea's default explicitly in every beforeEach rather than relying on clearAllMocks) and tests/fleet-status-version-check.test.ts (3, full fleetStatus() integration -- match/mismatch/throw across both compact and json formats, mocking version-check.js and kb-stats.js entirely so it never touches real version.json or kb.sqlite state). npm run build clean; byte-scanned changed files for non-ASCII -- check-status.ts's 9 hits are the pre-existing cloud-cost-warning unicode symbol, confirmed via git diff to be outside every line this task touched.

tasks.title: VERIFY Phase 2
tasks.notes: npm run build: CLEAN (tsc, no errors/output). npm test (npx vitest run, full suite): 135 test files, 1956 tests -- 1936 passed, 6 failed, 14 skipped. All 6 failures are on the allowed list, no new/different failures: (a) 2 timezone failures in tests/time-utils.test.ts (toLocalISOString offset/minute-preservation, yashr-302); (b) 4 pre-existing failures in tests/knowledge/kb-session-prime.test.ts 'kb_session_prime graph-neighbor expansion' describe block (graceful-skip x2, isError-context, FTS-hostile-neighbor) -- unmocked process.cwd() leaks this repo's real .fleet/kb-canonical.json into that describe block (yashr-bwc), unrelated to any Phase 2 change and unaffected by T2.1-T2.4 (none of which touch kb-session-prime.ts). npx gitnexus analyze: SUCCEEDED (20.2s, 4399 nodes, 11970 edges, 250 clusters, 300 flows, incremental changed=9 added=7); it modified AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md (injected non-ASCII gitnexus:start/end markers) as documented in the KB runbook -- reverted both via git checkout -- AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md immediately after, confirmed clean via git status --porcelain (only the pre-existing untracked .claude/skills/ and .claude/worktrees/ orchestrator-scratch directories remain, same as noted in T1.7). Pushing branch feat/code-intelligence-abstraction next (never main, no PR); commit field updated to this VERIFY commit's real SHA once created.
